macro_summary: Tuesday's session saw WTI surge 3.7% to $94.80 after Iran halted diplomatic exchanges and threatened a full Strait of Hormuz blockade, reversing earlier ceasefire optimism. Hawkish Fed rhetoric escalated as Cleveland Fed chief Hammack explicitly opened the door to rate hikes, with US10Y edging to 4.45%. The S&P 500 edged up 0.4% led by AI optimism, while SOXX rallied 6% on optics and networking exuberance.
commodities_note: Oil surged to $94.80 on Hormuz risk, its biggest single-day jump in weeks. Gold dipped 0.5% to $4,506. The dollar index was steady at 99.21. VIX eased to 15.77 despite the geopolitical escalation, signaling complacency in equities.

singapore.summary: All three banks remain overbought with RSI >74, extending their multi-week rally without any fresh catalyst. Structurally, the dual NIM headwind from elevated US rates (10Y at 4.45%) and MAS S$NEER appreciation continues to compress margins.
singapore.key_development: No new catalyst; the UOB-FPT partnership is an incremental positive but does not change the NIM dynamic.

semiconductors.summary: The cluster is uniformly extended with 11 of 11 semis in CAUTION, most extreme. SOXX surged 6% on optics and networking enthusiasm, but the price has run far ahead of fundamentals; the extreme divergence between memory (MU +96% monthly) and foundry names like TSEM signals caution.
semiconductors.key_development: LITE surged +12.6% on Marvell/NVIDIA optics enthusiasm; AVGO faces a high-bar earnings print tomorrow.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG offer the best risk/reward within 5% of their 50-day averages, while MSFT extends but holds trend exception. PLTR faces a policy headwind from the AI executive order but remains deeply overvalued. Alphabet's $80B raise reinforces capex commitment but dilutes.
big_tech.thesis_status: Mixed — pockets of value emerging as AI capex concentration risks weigh
big_tech.key_development: Alphabet's $80B equity raise and Microsoft's in-house MAI models signal a push to reduce OpenAI dependency.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: NVDA's matching $2B photonics investment partner alongside LITE; optics rally validates thesis.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back to $380 on profit-taking after LITE's surge.

interconnected.name: Marvell Technology
interconnected.reason: Custom ASIC design wins (Amazon Trainium) and NVDA CEO's 'next trillion-dollar' comment; direct read-through on AVGO's earnings tomorrow.
interconnected.entry_note: Reassess after AVGO earnings if MRVL holds above the 50-day on volume.

interconnected.name: Arista Networks
interconnected.reason: AI datacenter networking upgrades benefit as hyperscaler capex rises; NOK's AI networking thesis shares the same tailwind.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if ANET pulls back 8% from current levels, aligning with NVDA's photonics cycle.

active_risks: Iran halts US talks, threatens Strait of Hormuz closure, pushing WTI above $94.
Fed's Hammack signals rates may need to rise if inflation persists, with core inflation at 3.8%.
US Senator Warren calls for closing loophole allowing AI chip exports to Chinese firms' overseas units.
Eurozone inflation climbs to 3.2%, cementing ECB rate hike expectations.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil surge benefits energy but weighs on consumer; semis face both tailwinds (AI demand) and headwinds (rate fears, export controls). Singapore banks benefit from higher rates but NIM compression persists. The sharp move in SOXX masks underlying fragility from extreme extensions.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran's Tasnim reported talks halted; Hammack explicitly open to rate hike; Alphabet announces $80B raise.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold off on new entries until the VIX settles below 15 and the extension regime softens; consider starter positions only in WATCH names on pullbacks.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: 22 of 27 names are hard-blocked on technical extension; only three WATCH entries offer near-term entry potential with caveats.

watch_for_entry.note: Enter above $258.60 on volume, stop below $248.74. Wide-stop R:R 0.9:1 keeps at WATCH. Prime Day catalyst adds near-term interest.
Upgrades to BUY candidate on reclaim of $404.47, stop below $347.58. Wide-stop R:R 1.18:1 fails threshold. The $80B raise confirms capex commitment.
Upgrades on pullback to 50-day at $404.72 or break above $486.97. Non-OpenAI RPO is the clean read; MAI models reduce OpenAI dependency.
Upgrades on reclaim of $7.95 with volume; sizing to $7.47 structural support (R:R 0.7:1) — starter only. Uber Madrid pilot adds commercial traction.

calibration_insights.note: Block is output-only — assembled post-LLM from signal_evaluation_log outcomes and never injected into the analysis prompt. The underlying lesson stats are separately persisted into report_memory.json under lessons_learned and DO reach the next day's prompt via the memory Layer 3 calibration loop. Treat as a reader-facing self-knowledge summary.
